The royal capital of Angkor Thom, built by Jayavarman VII in the latter half of the 12th century, lies in the tropical forest of the Angkor Archaeological Park. The royal city, surrounded by a 3 km-square enclosure and moat, was developed as the center of the Angkor Empire’s expansive territory.
